element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns left-right movement and basic shooting in opening moments
Primary weapon throughout, establishes baseline damage
Defensive positioning tool, requires understanding of rear threats
Modified from Thunder Force II; player learns weapon switching and loss mechanics
Auto-grants maximum of two units; expands firepower by mirroring player weapon
Four-level speed setting; critical for dodging and positioning
Visual intimidation and pattern learning; documented as arcade-quality sprites
Only active weapon lost on default difficulty; all weapons lost on higher difficulties
Player chooses from Hydra, Gorgon, Seiren, Haides, Ellis; order affects strategy

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After all five cloaking device stages
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Linear progression; cumulative difficulty; final boss 'Cha Os' (bio-computer)

typical_relief_triggers: Brief safe zone between major encounters
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade-style intensity with escalating difficulty; horizontally scrolling format maintains constant forward pressure; large enemies create visual tension peaks; higher difficulty modes punish all weapon loss, intensifying resource management rhythm

difficulty_curve.early_game: Selectable starting planets allow player choice of difficulty sequence; baseline threat from large enemies and multiple weapon types introduced gradually
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy complexity after initial planet selection; pressure intensifies as player approaches later stages
difficulty_curve.late_game: Three-stage ORN headquarters sequence represents cumulative difficulty spike; final confrontation with bio-computer Cha Os; higher difficulty modes enforce complete weapon loss on any ship destruction
notable_spikes: Transition from individual planet stages to ORN headquarters linear sequence
Final stages with all five cloaking devices destroyed (ORN defenses fully activated)
Cha Os boss encounter (biological computer antagonist)

notable_level_patterns.description: Players choose from five planets (Hydra, Gorgon, Seiren, Haides, Ellis) in any order, each containing a cloaking device to destroy. This non-linear structure diverges from linear scrollers but maintains horizontal progression within each stage.
After all five cloaking devices destroyed, game forces linear progression through three ORN headquarters stages with no further player choice
Stages feature visually intricate, large-scale enemy sprites (documented as 'gorgeously rendered' and 'detailed'). These enemies establish stage identity and visual threat communication.

sources_used: wikipedia - Thunder Force III article (Thunder Spirits SNES port documentation, gameplay mechanics, stage structure, difficulty modes, level design changes)
